What to expect

What happens after you click Start My Quote.

No mystery, no phone tree — here's the actual timeline.

First 2 minutes

Tell us about the property

Complete the secure quote request online, or call and we'll take it down for you. No obligation either way.

Same business day

A licensed specialist compares your options

We review up to nine private flood markets plus the NFIP where appropriate, then walk you through price, limits, exclusions, waiting periods, and what your lender needs — including when the federal program is your better fit.

When you say go

Choose, bind, and we handle the paperwork

Pay online or have us bill your mortgage company; we send your lender everything they need. Coverage begins after carrier approval, binding confirmation, and payment — often the same day.

Independent advice

Private flood or NFIP? We compare both — honestly.

There is no one-size-fits-all answer. We look at your property, lender requirements, limits, and budget — then tell you plainly when the federal program is the better fit.

9+NFIPup to nine private flood markets, plus the NFIP when appropriate

Many clients save when we compare. Pricing varies by property, carrier, coverage, and eligibility — so we show you the tradeoffs, not just a number.

Typical differences we walk you through. NFIP figures per FEMA program rules; private terms vary by carrier and eligibility.
What we compareNFIPPrivate flood
Building limit (home)$250,000 capHigher limits often available
Waiting periodTypically 30 daysVaries — often shorter
Loss of use / living expensesNot coveredSometimes available
Replacement-cost optionsLimitedBroader options exist

Flood zones, demystified

Told you’re in a flood zone? Start with your zone’s guide.

Most people land here because a lender or escrow officer just named their zone. Every zone has a plain-English guide: what it means, whether coverage is required, and what drives your price.

Zone X Zone A Zone AE Zones AH & AO Zones V & VE Base Flood Elevation Lender requirements

What actually decides your premium

In plain English — no jargon required.

  1. Your flood zone — the risk level FEMA assigns your address
  2. Your building — rebuild cost, foundation, and first-floor height
  3. Your choices — coverage limits and the deductible you pick
  4. The carrier — the same house prices very differently market to market

Number 4 is why people call us. We compare up to nine private markets plus the NFIP, so you see your best available price — not one company's take-it-or-leave-it rate.

Design notes — not part of the site

How this concept works

Everything above is one screen-accurate homepage concept for the Divi → Kadence rebuild. Use the Preview toggle (bottom-right) to see the same system re-skinned for StatewideFloodInsurance.com — one design system, two brands.

Color tokens

Harbor navy #0A2540 — hero, footer, authority Pacific blue #0891C7 — brand cyan, made accessible Pacific bright #38C6F4 — accents on navy Action cyan #25C1EE — the brand cyan, reserved for quote CTAs Paper #F7FAFC / mist #E7F0F6 grounds Statewide teal #0E8E8A — sister-brand swap

Rule

The bright brand cyan appears only on things you can click to get a quote; supporting blues stay deeper and quieter, so the CTA is always the brightest element on screen. Review stars use Google's amber by convention — it is not a brand color.

Typography

Bricolage Grotesque (800) for display — confident and contemporary without being trendy. Public Sans (400/600) for UI and body — the U.S. government’s own typeface, a quiet nod to a FEMA/NFIP-adjacent business. Both are on Google Fonts; Kadence hosts them locally (two woff2 files, ~100 KB total).

Scale

  • H1 60/52/38 · H2 40–28 (clamped)
  • Body 16.5px / 1.65 · max 65ch
  • Labels 12px caps, +0.12em tracking

Hero video + section rhythm

Per DECISIONS.md your raindrop video is the CFI signature (embedded in this mock): 720p silent loop on desktop, a real video frame as static poster on mobile, data-saver, and reduced-motion, with a navy gradient guarding text contrast. Below it, sections alternate navy → mist → paper so each band reads distinctly while scrolling.

Motion

The video is the only thing that moves. No sliders, no carousels, no decorative animation, no jargon graphics.

Interior: service page

Slim hero: H1 + trust chips + quote card (persists)
What’s covered / not covered — two-column table
Rate factors for this property type
FAQ accordion (schema markup)
CTA band + waterline

The quote card follows the visitor down the page in a sticky rail on desktop.

Interior: zone / city SEO template

H1 pattern: “Flood Insurance in {Zone/City}” + zone badge
Key facts box: requirement, BFE, what drives price
Sample rate table (NFIP vs private)
Plain-English zone explainer · internal links to siblings
FAQ + FAQPage schema
Quote CTA

One Kadence template renders every zone and city page — content changes, layout doesn’t.

Interior: article (YMYL)

Byline: author photo · license # · reviewed date
Key takeaways box (their current pattern, kept)
65ch body · sticky TOC rail · sources cited
Author box + related guides
Soft CTA: “Check your rate”

E-E-A-T signals (byline, review date, license, sources) are template-level, so every article gets them.

Concept elementKadence implementation
Color tokensGlobal Palette slots 1–9 (navy, pacific, bright, gold, grounds, inks). Statewide = same child theme, different palette values.
FontsKadence local Google-font hosting: Bricolage Grotesque (headings), Public Sans (body). Two files, preloaded.
Utility bar + headerKadence Header Builder: top row (license/phone), main row (logo, nav, CTA button), sticky + transparent-off.
Hero + quote cardRow Layout (2-col); video via native <video> with poster + reduced-motion/mobile fallback. Card links to the existing Cognito quote page — "Start My Quote," never "instant quote."
Steps / coverage / guidesKadence Blocks: Info Box + Icon List; guides via Posts block pulling real articles.
NFIP comparison & zone lookupCore table block styled globally; lookup card is a styled link to the zone guides at launch (real FEMA API later). All claims follow DECISIONS.md qualification language.
ReviewsKadence Testimonials or a Google-reviews plugin, capped at 3 on home.
PerformanceNo video/sliders, text LCP, 2 font files, AVIF images with dimensions, Kadence CSS-per-page. Target: 95+ mobile PSI.
